sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

values.Description: Command Line Interface for Microsoft® Volume Shadow Copy Service
Command Line Interface for Microsoft\xae Volume Shadow Copy Service
Command Line Interface for Microsoft\xae Volume Shadow Copy Service

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This binary is a Babuk Ransomware variant (v3), designed to encrypt user data and demand payment. It employs several high-impact malicious behaviors: it inhibits system recovery by executing 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' via ShellExecuteW; it uses the Windows Restart Manager API (RmStartSession, RmGetList) to identify and terminate processes locking files to ensure successful encryption; and it terminates a predefined list of services and processes. The malware performs multi-threaded encryption on local drives and network shares (via WNetEnumResourceW), renames encrypted files with the '.babyk' extension, and drops a ransom note named 'How To Restore Your Files.txt'. Key IOCs include the mutex 'babuk_v3', the '.babyk' file extension, and a Tor-based ransom portal (babukq4e2p4wu4iq.onion).
